member.name: Catherine McKinnell
member.role: MP for Newcastle upon Tyne North
member.synopsis: Catherine McKinnell is the Labour MP for Newcastle upon Tyne North , and has been an MP continually since 6 May 2010.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Armed Forces: Housing
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what percentage of a) new build and b) repaired old military homes, have been constructed using timber since the publication of the Defence Housing Strategy in December 2025, and whether his Department is conside
writtenQuestions.answerText: Since the publication of the Defence Housing Strategy in November 2025, the Department has not purchased any new build Service Family Accommodation (SFA) of timber frame construction. In relation to the repair or refurbishment of SFA, the Department is in...

writtenQuestions.heading: Air Force: Helmets
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what steps are being taken to address delays to the Aircrew Helmet programme, and how he will make sure his department's approach to the procurement aligns with the objectives of the Defence Investment Plan to pr
writtenQuestions.answerText: The procurement strategy for the Aircrew Helmet Programme will align with the objectives of the Defence Investment Plan in promoting British manufacturing, sovereign capability, and SME participation, whilst also ensuring that the required safety and capa...

writtenQuestions.heading: Schools: Climate Change
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what steps her Department is taking to support schools to help tackle climate change.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Education Estates Strategy sets out plans for an education estate that supports opportunity for all, with an education estate that is safe, suitable, sustainable and appropriately sufficiently sized. It is backed by a ten-year plan to deliver a decade...

writtenQuestions.heading: Schools: Buildings
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, whether her Department has considered the potential merits of using timber materials in a) constructing and b) maintaining school buildings.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The government recognises that timber offers a solution as a renewable, low-carbon resource. It offers potential to reduce emissions and create jobs, as set out in the Timber in Construction Roadmap: https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/timber-in-co...

writtenQuestions.heading: Armed Forces: Housing
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, whether his Department has considered the potential merits of using timber to a) build new and b) repair old military homes.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Defence Housing Strategy published on 3 November 2025, sets out a vision for the future transformation of military homes through a record £9 billion investment over a decade. The Department is in the early stages of the renovation programme. As we loo...

writtenQuestions.heading: Obesity: Health Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps his Department is taking to reduce waiting lists for NHS-funded weight loss programmes.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The National Health Service provides a range of weight management services, and we recognise that waiting times for some programmes can be long. NHS England is taking steps to improve access. Access to the NHS Digital Weight Management Programme is being ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Clothing: Manufacturing Industries
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, if her Department will make an assessment of the potential merits of fashion watchdog.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Office for Responsible Business Conduct promotes the OECD Guidelines for Multinational Enterprises on Responsible Business Conduct and provides a non-judicial grievance mechanism for complaints of non-observance by UK businesses. The government also l...

writtenQuestions.heading: Clothing: Manufacturing Industries
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what steps the Department are taking to help tackle abusive purchasing practices in the fashion industry.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Office for Responsible Business Conduct promotes the OECD Guidelines for Multinational Enterprises on Responsible Business Conduct and provides a non-judicial grievance mechanism for complaints of non-observance by UK businesses. The government also l...

writtenQuestions.heading: Groceries Code Adjudicator
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what steps the Government is taking to expand the remit of the Groceries Code Adjudicator to tackle unfair trading practices in supply chains.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is undertaking the fourth statutory review into the effectiveness of the GCA, and covers the period from 31 March 2022 - 1 April 2025. The review sought input on the GCA's effectiveness in enforcing the Code, its role alongside the Agricult...

writtenQuestions.heading: Groceries Code Adjudicator: Agricultural Supply Chain Adjudicator
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, if his Department will make an assessment of the potential merits of expanding the scope of the independent Groceries Code Adjudicator to include the functions of the Agricultural Supply Chain Adjudica
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is undertaking the fourth statutory review into the effectiveness of the GCA, and covers the period from 31 March 2022 – 1 April 2025. The review sought input on the GCA’s effectiveness in enforcing the Code, its role alongside the Agricult...

writtenQuestions.heading: HM Passport Office
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, if she will review HM Passport Office's policy on considering urgent government business or compassionate reasons to include (a) scattering of ashes and (b) ritual and prayer ceremonies.
writtenQuestions.answerText: HM Passport Office policy already allows applications to be considered under compassionate grounds where there is a time-critical requirement arising from religious observance following a death. This includes the scattering of ashes or ritual and prayer c...

writtenQuestions.heading: Erasmus+ Programme: Schools
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, whether she is taking steps to include schools in the Erasmus+ programme.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The department has agreed to work towards association to Erasmus+, on mutually agreed financial terms. Negotiations are under way, and the terms of association are subject to further discussions.The current Erasmus+ programme is open to a broad audience a...

writtenQuestions.heading: Erasmus+ Programme: Schools
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what contingency plans his Department has in place to support schools during the transition period from the Turing Scheme to Erasmus+.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The UK and EU have agreed to work towards association to the Erasmus+ programme, on mutually agreed financial terms. Negotiations are underway and the terms of any association will be subject to further discussions. The department will have to consider th...

writtenQuestions.heading: Turing Scheme: Schools
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, whether she is taking steps to simplify the process for Turing Scheme applications for schools.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Since bringing management of the Turing Scheme in-house for the 2024/25 academic year, in response to feedback from the education sector, the department introduced a streamlined digital application service that was co-designed. The service also includes s...

writtenQuestions.heading: Turing Scheme: Schools
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what steps she is taking to support schools in disadvantaged areas with applications to the Turing scheme; and what assessment she has made of the trends in the level of concern that the current design of the a
writtenQuestions.answerText: In order to support schools, especially those in disadvantaged areas, the department provided guidance and webinars to enable potential applicants to find out more about the Turing Scheme, what it could offer their students and how to make their applicati...

writtenQuestions.heading: Turing Scheme: Educational Visits
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what steps she is taking to reduce the risk of schools in disadvantaged areas excluding pupils from Turing Scheme-funded excursions due to the cap on funding.
writtenQuestions.answerText: In the current academic year of the Turing Scheme, we applied a cap to the funding that schools could apply for. Schools could receive funding of up to £50,000 for a single application or, applying as a consortium, up to £50,000 per school to a maximum of...

writtenQuestions.heading: Turing Scheme: Schools
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what steps she is taking to ensure that Turing grants to schools take into consideration the additional costs of (a) travel, (b) appropriate (i) clothing and (ii) equipment and (c) other items for children and
writtenQuestions.answerText: Turing Scheme funding is a contribution towards the costs of international educational placements. The scheme provides a contribution to the travel and living costs of students and accompanying staff. Additionally, the Turing Scheme provides readiness to ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Energy Company Obligation: Fraud
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what discussions he has had with Ofgem on the adequacy of its counter-fraud policies, in the context of the National Audit Office’s report titled Energy efficiency installations under the Ene
writtenQuestions.answerText: Ofgem are the administrator for both ECO4 and GBIS and there is regular engagement between Ofgem and DESNZ on all aspects of the schemes. In regard to the NAO report, Ofgem are actively engaged in the review of evidence relating to possible fraud and they...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Warm Homes Plan
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what steps he is taking to review (a) regulations and (b) consumer protections through the Warm Homes Plan.
writtenQuestions.answerText: This government is committed to protecting consumers undertaking home retrofit work, as well as improving the overall consumer journey. We are leading reform of a system which is clearly not fit for purpose. This reform will involve rapidly changing the l...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Council Tax: Reform
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, if she will reform the current council tax system to reduce localised disparities in property valuations.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The valuation of all properties in England is carried out by the Valuation Office Agency (VOA), who undertake this role independently of ministers. The Chancellor makes tax policy decisions at fiscal events. The Government remains committed to keeping all...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government
